question_text: Show that the differential equation {tex}y d x + x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| d y - 2 x d y = 0{/tex} is homogeneous and solve it.
question_html: Show that the differential equation {tex}y d x + x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| d y - 2 x d y = 0{/tex} is homogeneous and solve it.
solution_text: According to the question , Given differential equation is {tex}y d x + x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| d y - 2 x d y = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad y d x = \left[ 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| \right] d y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { y } { 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| }{/tex} Now, let {tex}F ( x , y ) = \frac { y } { 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| }{/tex} On replace x by {tex}\lambda x{/tex} and y by {tex}\lambda y{/tex} both sides, we get {tex}F ( \lambda x , \lambda y ) = \frac { \lambda y } { 2 \lambda x - \lambda x \log \left| \frac { \lambda y } { \lambda x } \right| }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \lambda y } { \lambda \left[ 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| \right] }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow F ( \lambda x , \lambda y ) = \lambda ^ { 0 } \frac { y } { 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| } = \lambda ^ { 0 } F ( x , y ){/tex} So, the given differential equation is homogeneous. On putting y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = v + x \frac { d v } { d x }{/tex} in Eq. (i). we get {tex}v + x \frac { d v } { d x } = \frac { v x } { 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { v x } { x } \right| } = \frac { v } { 2 - \log | v | }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac { d v } { d x } = \frac { v } { 2 - \log | v | } - v = \frac { v - 2 v + v \log | v | } { 2 - \log | v | }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac { d v } { d x } = \frac { - v + v \log | v | } { 2 - \log | v | }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { 2 - \log | v | } { v \log | v | - v } d v = \frac { d x } { x }{/tex} On integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \frac { 2 - \log | v | } { v ( \log | y | - 1 ) } d v = \int \frac { d x } { x }{/tex} On putting log |v| = t {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { v } d v = d t{/tex} Then, {tex}\int \frac { 2 - t } { t - 1 } d t = \log | x | + C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \left( \frac { 1 } { t - 1 } - 1 \right) d t = \log | x | + C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \log | t - 1 | - t = \log | x | + C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \log | \log v - 1 | - \log v = \log | x | + C{/tex} [put t = log |v|] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \log \left| \frac { \log v - 1 } { v } \right| = \log | x | + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \log m - \log n = \log \left( \frac { m } { n } \right) \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log \left| \frac { \log v - 1 } { v } \right| - \log | x | = C \Rightarrow \log \left| \frac { \log v - 1 } { v x } \right| = C{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \log \left| \frac { \log \frac { y } { x } - 1 } { y } \right| = c \left[ \because y = v x \Rightarrow v = \frac { y } { x } \right]{/tex} which is the required solution.
solution_html:
According to the question ,
Given differential equation is
{tex}y d x + x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| d y - 2 x d y = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad y d x = \left[ 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| \right] d y{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { y } { 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| }{/tex}
Now, let{tex}F ( x , y ) = \frac { y } { 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| }{/tex}
On replace x by {tex}\lambda x{/tex} and y by {tex}\lambda y{/tex} both sides, we get
{tex}F ( \lambda x , \lambda y ) = \frac { \lambda y } { 2 \lambda x - \lambda x \log \left| \frac { \lambda y } { \lambda x } \right| }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { \lambda y } { \lambda \left[ 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| \right] }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow F ( \lambda x , \lambda y ) = \lambda ^ { 0 } \frac { y } { 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { y } { x } \right| } = \lambda ^ { 0 } F ( x , y ){/tex}
So, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
On putting y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = v + x \frac { d v } { d x }{/tex} in Eq. (i).
we get {tex}v + x \frac { d v } { d x } = \frac { v x } { 2 x - x \log \left| \frac { v x } { x } \right| } = \frac { v } { 2 - \log | v | }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac { d v } { d x } = \frac { v } { 2 - \log | v | } - v = \frac { v - 2 v + v \log | v | } { 2 - \log | v | }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac { d v } { d x } = \frac { - v + v \log | v | } { 2 - \log | v | }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { 2 - \log | v | } { v \log | v | - v } d v = \frac { d x } { x }{/tex}
On integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int \frac { 2 - \log | v | } { v ( \log | y | - 1 ) } d v = \int \frac { d x } { x }{/tex}
On putting log |v| = t {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { v } d v = d t{/tex}
Then, {tex}\int \frac { 2 - t } { t - 1 } d t = \log | x | + C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \left( \frac { 1 } { t - 1 } - 1 \right) d t = \log | x | + C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \log | t - 1 | - t = \log | x | + C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \log | \log v - 1 | - \log v = \log | x | + C{/tex} [put t = log |v|]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \log \left| \frac { \log v - 1 } { v } \right| = \log | x | + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \log m - \log n = \log \left( \frac { m } { n } \right) \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \log \left| \frac { \log v - 1 } { v } \right| - \log | x | = C \Rightarrow \log \left| \frac { \log v - 1 } { v x } \right| = C{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \log \left| \frac { \log \frac { y } { x } - 1 } { y } \right| = c \left[ \because y = v x \Rightarrow v = \frac { y } { x } \right]{/tex}
which is the required solution.

question_html: Show that the differential equation {tex}\left(1+e^{\frac{x}{y}}\right) d x+e^{\frac{x}{y}}\left(1-\frac{x}{y}\right) d y=0{/tex} is homogeneous and solve it.
solution_text: We have {tex}\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dx}+\mathrm{e^x} ^/\mathrm{^y}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dy}=0{/tex} {tex}\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dx}=-\mathrm{e^x} \mathrm{^{/y}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dy}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-e^{x / y}\left(1-\frac{x}{y}\right)}{\left(1+e^{x / y}\right)}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{-e^{x / y}\left(1-\frac{x}{y}\right)}{\left(1+e^{x / y}\right)}{/tex} Here, putting x = kx and y = ky {tex}\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=\frac{-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{kx} / \mathrm{ky}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{kx}}{\mathrm{ky}}\right)}{\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{kx} / \mathrm{ky}}\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right)}{\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right)}{/tex} = k 0 f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. {tex}\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dx}+\mathrm{e}^{\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dy}=0{/tex} {tex}\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dx}=-\mathrm{e}^{\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dy}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{-e^{x / y}\left(1-\frac{x}{y}\right)}{\left(1+e^{x / y}\right)}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. x = vy Differentiation above equation with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=v+y \frac{d v}{d y}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{y} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dy}}=\frac{-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{vy} / \mathrm{y}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{vy}}{\mathrm{y}}\right)}{(1+\mathrm{ev} / \mathrm{y})}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{y} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dy}}=\frac{-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}(1-\mathrm{v})}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+\mathrm{v}} \mathrm{dv}=-\frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \mathrm{dy}{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \frac{1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+\mathrm{v}} \mathrm{dv}=\int \frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{y}{/tex} ......(i) Let {tex}I_{1}=\int \frac{1+e^{v}}{e^{v}+v} d v{/tex} Put e v + v = t (e v + 1)dv = dt {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+1=\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dv}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{dv}=\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+1}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} log t log(e v + v) {tex}\therefore{/tex} log(e v + v) = - logy + logC ( {tex}\therefore{/tex} From (i) eq.) {tex}\log \left(e^{x / y}+\frac{x}{y}\right)=-\log y+\log C{/tex} {tex}\log \left(\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right)=\log \frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} Multiply by y on both side, we get ye x/y + x = C x + ye x/y = C The required solution of the differential equation.
solution_html:
We have 
{tex}\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dx}+\mathrm{e^x} ^/\mathrm{^y}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dy}=0{/tex} 
{tex}\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dx}=-\mathrm{e^x} \mathrm{^{/y}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dy}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-e^{x / y}\left(1-\frac{x}{y}\right)}{\left(1+e^{x / y}\right)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{-e^{x / y}\left(1-\frac{x}{y}\right)}{\left(1+e^{x / y}\right)}{/tex} 
Here, putting x = kx and y = ky
{tex}\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=\frac{-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{kx} / \mathrm{ky}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{kx}}{\mathrm{ky}}\right)}{\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{kx} / \mathrm{ky}}\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right)}{\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right)}{/tex} 
= k0f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
{tex}\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dx}+\mathrm{e}^{\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dy}=0{/tex} 
{tex}\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dx}=-\mathrm{e}^{\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right) \mathrm{dy}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{-e^{x / y}\left(1-\frac{x}{y}\right)}{\left(1+e^{x / y}\right)}{/tex} 
To solve it we make the substitution.
x = vy
Differentiation above equation with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=v+y \frac{d v}{d y}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{y} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dy}}=\frac{-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{vy} / \mathrm{y}}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{vy}}{\mathrm{y}}\right)}{(1+\mathrm{ev} / \mathrm{y})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{y} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dy}}=\frac{-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}(1-\mathrm{v})}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+\mathrm{v}} \mathrm{dv}=-\frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \mathrm{dy}{/tex}
Integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int \frac{1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+\mathrm{v}} \mathrm{dv}=\int \frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{y}{/tex} ......(i)
Let {tex}I_{1}=\int \frac{1+e^{v}}{e^{v}+v} d v{/tex} 
Put ev + v = t
(ev + 1)dv = dt
{tex}\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+1=\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dv}}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{dv}=\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+1}{/tex} 
{tex}\int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} 
log t
log(ev + v)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} log(ev + v) = - logy + logC ({tex}\therefore{/tex} From (i) eq.) 
{tex}\log \left(e^{x / y}+\frac{x}{y}\right)=-\log y+\log C{/tex} 
{tex}\log \left(\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}\right)=\log \frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x} / \mathrm{y}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} 
Multiply by y on both side, we get
yex/y + x = C
x + yex/y = C
The required solution of the differential equation.

question_text: Show that the differential equation (x + y) dy + (x - y) dx = 0, is homogenous and find the particular solution, given that y = 1 when x = 1.
question_html: Show that the differential equation (x + y) dy + (x - y) dx = 0, is homogenous and find the particular solution, given that y = 1 when x = 1.
solution_text: (x + y)dy + (x - y)dx = 0 {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} Here, putting x= kx and y = ky {tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=-\frac{(\mathrm{kx}-\mathrm{ky})}{(\mathrm{kx}+\mathrm{ky})}{/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. Now, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx ...(i) Differentiating equation (i), with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{vx})}{(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{vx})}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(1-\mathrm{v})}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(1-\mathrm{v})}{(1+\mathrm{v})}-\mathrm{v}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1+\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}^{2}}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1-\mathrm{v}^{2}}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-\left(1+\mathrm{v}^{2}\right)}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1+v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \frac{1+v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{1+v^{2}} d v+\int \frac{v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\tan ^{-1} v+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+v^{2}\right)=-\log x+C{/tex} {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log x+C{/tex} y = 1 when x = 1 {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{1}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{1}{1}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log 1+C{/tex} {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2=0+c{/tex} {tex}C=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} {tex}\therefore \tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} where, {tex}C=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} {tex}\therefore \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right){/tex} = {tex}-\log x+\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} {tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}}\right){/tex} = {tex}-2 \log x+\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex} {tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}}\right)+\log x^{2}=\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex} {tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex} The required solution of the differential equation.
solution_html:
(x + y)dy + (x - y)dx = 0
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} 
Here, putting x= kx and y = ky
{tex}f(\mathrm{kx}, \mathrm{ky})=-\frac{(\mathrm{kx}-\mathrm{ky})}{(\mathrm{kx}+\mathrm{ky})}{/tex} = k0.f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
Now,
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{(x-y)}{(x+y)}{/tex} 
To solve it we make the substitution.
y = vx ...(i)
Differentiating equation (i), with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{vx})}{(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{vx})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(1-\mathrm{v})}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{(1-\mathrm{v})}{(1+\mathrm{v})}-\mathrm{v}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1+\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}^{2}}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1-\mathrm{v}^{2}}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-\left(1+\mathrm{v}^{2}\right)}{(1+\mathrm{v})}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1+v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
Integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int \frac{1+v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\int \frac{1}{1+v^{2}} d v+\int \frac{v}{1+v^{2}} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\tan ^{-1} v+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+v^{2}\right)=-\log x+C{/tex} 
{tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log x+C{/tex} 
y = 1 when x = 1
 {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{1}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{1}{1}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log 1+C{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2=0+c{/tex} 
{tex}C=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)^{2}\right)=-\log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
where, {tex}C=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}-\log x+\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} 
{tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}-2 \log x+\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex}
{tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}}\right)+\log x^{2}=\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex} 
{tex}2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2{/tex} 
The required solution of the differential equation.

question_text: Show that the differential equation {tex}x^{2} d y+\left(x y+y^{2}\right) d x=0{/tex} is homogenous and find the particular solution, given that y = 1 when x = 1.
question_html: Show that the differential equation  {tex}x^{2} d y+\left(x y+y^{2}\right) d x=0{/tex} is homogenous and  find the particular solution, given that y = 1 when x = 1.
solution_text: We have x 2 dy + (xy + y 2 )dx = 0 {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{\left(x y+y^{2}\right)}{x^{2}}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=-\frac{\left(x y+y^{2}\right)}{x^{2}}{/tex} Here, putting x = kx and y = ky {tex}f(k x, k y)=-\frac{\left(k x k y+k^{2} y^{2}\right)}{k^{2} x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{k}^{2}}{\mathrm{k}^{2}} \cdot-\frac{\left(\mathrm{xy}+\mathrm{y}^{2}\right)}{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. x 2 dy + (xy + y 2 )dx = 0 {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{\left(x y+y^{2}\right)}{x^{2}}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx Differentiating above equation with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\left(\mathrm{x} \cdot \mathrm{vx}+\mathrm{v}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2}\right)}{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\left(\mathrm{vx}^{2}+\mathrm{v}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2}\right)}{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}^{2}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\mathrm{v}(\mathrm{v}+2){/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{v(v+2)} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \frac{1}{v(v+2)} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2}{v(v+2)} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2+v-v}{v(v+2)} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\left(\frac{2+v}{v(v+2)}-\frac{v}{v(v+2)}\right) d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\left(\frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{v+2}\right) d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}(\log v-\log (v+2))=-\log x+\log C{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\log \frac{v}{v+2}\right)=\log \frac{C}{x}{/tex} {tex}\log \left(\frac{\frac{y}{x}}{\frac{y}{x}+2}\right)=2 \log \frac{c}{x}{/tex} {tex}\log \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{y}+2 \mathrm{x}}\right)=\log \left(\frac{\mathrm{c}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)^{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{y}+2 \mathrm{x}}=\left(\frac{\mathrm{c}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)^{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{x^{2} y}{y+2 x}=c^{2}{/tex} y = 1 when x = 1 {tex}C^{2}=\frac{1}{1+2}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{x^{2} y}{y+2 x}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} 3x 2 y = y + 2x y + 2x = 3x 2 y The required solution of the differential equation.
solution_html:
We have
x2dy + (xy + y2)dx = 0
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{\left(x y+y^{2}\right)}{x^{2}}{/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=-\frac{\left(x y+y^{2}\right)}{x^{2}}{/tex} 
Here, putting x = kx and y = ky
{tex}f(k x, k y)=-\frac{\left(k x k y+k^{2} y^{2}\right)}{k^{2} x^{2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\mathrm{k}^{2}}{\mathrm{k}^{2}} \cdot-\frac{\left(\mathrm{xy}+\mathrm{y}^{2}\right)}{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} 
= k0.f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
x2dy + (xy + y2)dx = 0
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{\left(x y+y^{2}\right)}{x^{2}}{/tex} 
To solve it we make the substitution.
y = vx
Differentiating above equation with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\left(\mathrm{x} \cdot \mathrm{vx}+\mathrm{v}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2}\right)}{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\frac{\left(\mathrm{vx}^{2}+\mathrm{v}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2}\right)}{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\mathrm{v}(\mathrm{v}+2){/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{v(v+2)} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
Integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int \frac{1}{v(v+2)} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2}{v(v+2)} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2+v-v}{v(v+2)} d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\left(\frac{2+v}{v(v+2)}-\frac{v}{v(v+2)}\right) d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\left(\frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{v+2}\right) d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{2}(\log v-\log (v+2))=-\log x+\log C{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\log \frac{v}{v+2}\right)=\log \frac{C}{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\log \left(\frac{\frac{y}{x}}{\frac{y}{x}+2}\right)=2 \log \frac{c}{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\log \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{y}+2 \mathrm{x}}\right)=\log \left(\frac{\mathrm{c}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{y}+2 \mathrm{x}}=\left(\frac{\mathrm{c}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{x^{2} y}{y+2 x}=c^{2}{/tex} 
y = 1 when x = 1
{tex}C^{2}=\frac{1}{1+2}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \frac{x^{2} y}{y+2 x}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} 
3x2y = y + 2x
y + 2x = 3x2y
The required solution of the differential equation.

question_text: Show that the differential equation {tex}\left[ x \sin ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { y } { x } \right) - y \right] d x + x d y = 0{/tex} is homogenous and find the particular solution, given that {tex}y = \frac { \pi } { 4 }{/tex} , when x = 1.
question_html: Show that the differential equation {tex}\left[ x \sin ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { y } { x } \right) - y \right] d x + x d y = 0{/tex} is homogenous and  find the particular solution, given that {tex}y = \frac { \pi } { 4 }{/tex}, when x = 1.
solution_text: [x sin 2 ( {tex}\frac yx{/tex} ) − y]dx + xdy = 0 xdy = -[x sin 2 ( {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} ) − y]dx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = - {tex}\frac{[x \sin ^2(\frac{y}{x})-y]}{x}{/tex} ....(1) F(x, y) = - {tex}\frac{[x \sin ^2(\frac{y}{x})-y]}{x}{/tex} F(tx, ty) = {tex}\frac{-\left(t x \sin ^{2}(\frac{t y} {t x})-t y\right)}{t x}{/tex} = t 0 F(x, y) Hence F(x, y) is a homogenous function of zero degree. Now put y = vx in (1), v + x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-(x \sin ^2v-vx)}{x}{/tex} Therefore v + x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = -sin 2 v + v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = -sin 2 v + v - v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = -sin 2 v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\large\frac{dv}{\sin ^2 v}=-\frac{dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cosec 2 v dv {tex}=\large\frac{-dx}{x}{/tex} Integrating on both sides, {tex}\int cosec ^2 v\; dv=-\int \large\frac{dx}{x}{/tex} -cot v = -log x - log c Now substituting for v, -cot {tex}\left( {\frac{y}{x}} \right){/tex} = -(log cx) cot {tex}\left( {\frac{y}{x}} \right){/tex} = log cx {tex}e^{\cot (y/x)}=cx{/tex} When y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} and x = 1, cot {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} = 1, so e 1 = c Hence e cot( {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} ) = ex {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}e^{\cot (x / y)-1}{/tex} = x
solution_html:
[x sin2({tex}\frac yx{/tex}) − y]dx + xdy = 0
xdy = -[x sin2({tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}) − y]dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = -{tex}\frac{[x \sin ^2(\frac{y}{x})-y]}{x}{/tex} ....(1)
F(x, y) = -{tex}\frac{[x \sin ^2(\frac{y}{x})-y]}{x}{/tex}
F(tx, ty) = {tex}\frac{-\left(t x \sin ^{2}(\frac{t y} {t x})-t y\right)}{t x}{/tex}
= t0 F(x, y)
Hence F(x, y) is a homogenous function of zero degree.
Now put y = vx in (1),
v + x{tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-(x \sin ^2v-vx)}{x}{/tex}
Therefore v + x{tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = -sin2v + v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x{tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = -sin2v + v - v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x{tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = -sin2v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\large\frac{dv}{\sin ^2 v}=-\frac{dx}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cosec2v dv {tex}=\large\frac{-dx}{x}{/tex}
Integrating on both sides,
{tex}\int cosec ^2 v\; dv=-\int \large\frac{dx}{x}{/tex}
-cot v = -log x - log c
Now substituting for v,
-cot{tex}\left( {\frac{y}{x}} \right){/tex} = -(log cx)
cot{tex}\left( {\frac{y}{x}} \right){/tex} = log cx
{tex}e^{\cot (y/x)}=cx{/tex}
When y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} and x = 1,
cot{tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} = 1, so e1 = c
Hence ecot({tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}) = ex
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}e^{\cot (x / y)-1}{/tex} = x

question_text: Show that the differential equation {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{y}{x}+cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0{/tex} is homogenous and find the particular solution, given that y = 0 when x = 1.
question_html: Show that the differential equation {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{y}{x}+cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0{/tex} is homogenous and find the particular solution, given that y = 0 when x = 1.
solution_text: We have {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{y}{x}+cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}-cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y}{x}-cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} Here, putting x= kx and y = ky {tex}f(k x, k y)=\frac{k y}{k x}-cosec \left(\frac{k y}{k x}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{y}{x}-cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{y}{x}+cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}-cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{vx}}{\mathrm{x}}-cosec \left(\frac{\mathrm{vx}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{v}-\operatorname{cosecv}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-cosec{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{cosec\ v} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \sin v d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} - cosv = - logx + C {tex}-\cos \frac{y}{x}=-\log x+C{/tex} y = 0 when x = 1 {tex}-\cos \frac{0}{1}=-\log 1+C{/tex} - 1 = C {tex}\therefore-\cos \frac{y}{x}=-\log x-1{/tex} {tex}\cos \frac{y}{x}=\log x+\log e{/tex} {tex}\cos \frac{y}{x}=\log |e x|{/tex} The required solution of the differential equation.
solution_html:
We have
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{y}{x}+cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}-cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y}{x}-cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} 
Here, putting x= kx and y = ky
{tex}f(k x, k y)=\frac{k y}{k x}-cosec \left(\frac{k y}{k x}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{y}{x}-cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} 
= k0.f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{y}{x}+cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}-cosec \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} 
To solve it we make the substitution.
y = vx
Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{vx}}{\mathrm{x}}-cosec \left(\frac{\mathrm{vx}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{v}-\operatorname{cosecv}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-cosec{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{cosec\ v} d v=-\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
Integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int \sin v d v=-\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} 
- cosv = - logx + C
{tex}-\cos \frac{y}{x}=-\log x+C{/tex} 
y = 0 when x = 1
{tex}-\cos \frac{0}{1}=-\log 1+C{/tex} 
- 1 = C
{tex}\therefore-\cos \frac{y}{x}=-\log x-1{/tex} 
{tex}\cos \frac{y}{x}=\log x+\log e{/tex} 
{tex}\cos \frac{y}{x}=\log |e x|{/tex} 
The required solution of the differential equation.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Show that the differential equation {tex}2 x y+y^{2}-2 x^{2} \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} , is homogenous and find the particular solution, given that y = 2 when x = 1.
question_html: Show that the differential equation {tex}2 x y+y^{2}-2 x^{2} \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex}, is homogenous and find the particular solution, given that y = 2 when x = 1.
solution_text: We have {tex}2 x y+y^{2}-2 x^{2} \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2 x y+y^{2}}{2 x^{2}}{/tex} Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{2 x y+y^{2}}{2 x^{2}}{/tex} Here, putting x = kx and y = ky {tex}f(k x, k y)=\frac{2 k x k y+(k y)^{2}}{2(k x)^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{k}^{2}}{\mathrm{k}^{2}} \cdot \frac{2 \mathrm{xy}+\mathrm{y}^{2}}{2 \mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} = k 0 .f(x,y) Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. {tex}2 \mathrm{xy}+\mathrm{y}^{2}-2 \mathrm{x}^{2} \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=0{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2 x y+y^{2}}{2 x^{2}}{/tex} To solve it we make the substitution. y = vx Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{2 \mathrm{x} \cdot \mathrm{vx}+(\mathrm{vx})^{2}}{2 \mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{2 v x^{2}+v^{2} x^{2}}{2 x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{2 v+v^{2}}{2}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{v}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{v}^{2}{/tex} {tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1}{2} v^{2}{/tex} {tex}2 \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}^{2}} \mathrm{dv}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int 2 \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}^{2}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}=\int \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}-\frac{2}{\mathrm{v}}=\log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}-\frac{2}{\mathrm{y} / \mathrm{x}}=\log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}-\frac{2 x}{y}=\log x+c{/tex} y = 2 when x = 1 {tex}-\frac{2 \times 1}{2}=\log 1+c{/tex} - 1 = C {tex}\therefore-\frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}=\log \mathrm{x}-1{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x}{y}=1-\log x{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{2 x}{1-\log |x|}: x \neq e, x \neq 0{/tex} The required solution of the differential equation.
solution_html:
We have
{tex}2 x y+y^{2}-2 x^{2} \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2 x y+y^{2}}{2 x^{2}}{/tex} 
Let {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{2 x y+y^{2}}{2 x^{2}}{/tex} 
Here, putting x = kx and y = ky
{tex}f(k x, k y)=\frac{2 k x k y+(k y)^{2}}{2(k x)^{2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\mathrm{k}^{2}}{\mathrm{k}^{2}} \cdot \frac{2 \mathrm{xy}+\mathrm{y}^{2}}{2 \mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} 
= k0.f(x,y)
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
{tex}2 \mathrm{xy}+\mathrm{y}^{2}-2 \mathrm{x}^{2} \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=0{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2 x y+y^{2}}{2 x^{2}}{/tex} 
To solve it we make the substitution.
y = vx
Differentiating eq. with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{2 \mathrm{x} \cdot \mathrm{vx}+(\mathrm{vx})^{2}}{2 \mathrm{x}^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{2 v x^{2}+v^{2} x^{2}}{2 x^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{2 v+v^{2}}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{x} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{v}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{v}^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1}{2} v^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}^{2}} \mathrm{dv}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
Integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int 2 \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}^{2}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}=\int \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{2}{\mathrm{v}}=\log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{2}{\mathrm{y} / \mathrm{x}}=\log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{2 x}{y}=\log x+c{/tex} 
y = 2 when x = 1
{tex}-\frac{2 \times 1}{2}=\log 1+c{/tex} 
- 1 = C
{tex}\therefore-\frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}=\log \mathrm{x}-1{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{2 x}{y}=1-\log x{/tex} 
{tex}y=\frac{2 x}{1-\log |x|}: x \neq e, x \neq 0{/tex} 
The required solution of the differential equation.
